title: Pomp and Circumstance: The Challenge and Complexity of Engaging India
summary: "...[I]t was important to reassure India that America is sensitive to New Delhi's role in the world and respects India's traditions and achievements. At a time of bilateral engagement, the president's visit has further contributed to creating a more productive working environment for negotiators from both sides. In addition, the inclination of Indian decision makers to take countermeasures, if they deem their pride to be hurt, should have lessened, which might prevent future setbacks."
body: Pundits, scholars and even decision makers following President Obama's visit to India might have gotten the impression that the president's trip was a little short on substance. India's prime minister, Narendra Modi, had invited Barack Obama to attend the colorful Republic Day Parade , which celebrates the adoption of India's constitution. Prior to attending the parade, which was enough of a show in itself, President Obama also paid a visit to the shrine that holds Mahatma Gandhi's ashes. A stop-over at the Taj Mahal, India's renowned monument, was cancelled because the president wanted to attend King Abdullah's funeral. In light of this schedule, one inevitably has to wonder whether symbolism was valued over substance....
